ref: Jacob went closer. His father touched him and said, “You sound like Jacob, but your hands feel hairy like Esau's.”
text: Ka Yaakubu daa saɣisi mir’ o ba Yisahaku, ka o shih’ o ka yɛli, Kukoli ŋɔ nyɛla Yaakubu kukoli, amaa nuhi ŋɔ nyɛla Iso nuhi.
translated: When Jacob was angry with Isaac, he said that he would go and pray; the children are like Jacob’s children, and the women are like Isaac’s women.

ref: including Hamor and Shechem. Then they took Dinah and left.
text: Bɛ daa ku Hamɔr min’ o bia Shɛkɛm gba, ka zaŋ Daina Shɛkɛm yiŋa n-chaŋ bɛ chandi.
translated: We will go to Hamor’s house, and then Daina will not be at school today.

ref: Hamor said to Jacob and his sons: My son Shechem really loves Dinah. Please let him marry her.
text: Ka Hamɔr daa yɛl’ o, “M bia Shɛkɛm bɔr’ a bipuɣiŋga, n suhir’ a, zaŋm’ o ti o ka o niŋ o paɣa.
translated: When Hamor said, “I will go to the market and bring something for you.”

ref: they said: Let's build a city with a tower that reaches to the sky! We'll use hard bricks and tar instead of stone and mortar. We'll become famous, and we won't be scattered all over the world.
text: Ka bɛ daa yɛl’ taba, “Kamiyana ka ti me timaŋmaŋ’ tiŋa, ka me pii tam di zuɣu ka di du hal ka di zuɣusaa ti tɔri sagbana n-zali yuli, dama di yi pa lala, ti ni ti wurim gili dunia ŋɔ zaa.”
translated: When I was young, “My friend, you gave me a gift again, and I went to the market, bought something and sold it at a higher price, but I was not happy, because you think the world is not good.”

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Naawuni daa nam ninsala ŋ-ŋmahindila omaŋa, ka o be kaman Naawuni ni be shɛm. O daa nam ba la doo mini paɣa.
translated: Naawuni is the one who always helps us, and he also works with Naawuni and his family. He also lives with his children.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Nuhu zuliya m-bɔŋɔ: Nuhu daa nyɛla wuntizɔra ŋun jɛmdi Naawuni, ka ka galimi o ʒiɛman’ ni.
translated: Nuhu says: Nuhu is very pleased to meet Naawuni, and he will help him.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli Ibrahimma, “Di simdi ni a min’ a zuliya maa gbibi n daalikauli maa bɛ ʒiɛmana zaa ni.
translated: When Naawuni went to Ibrahim, he said, “If you don’t give me what you have taken, I won’t be able to finish the work.”

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Amaa o daa bi zaŋdi dari maa sɔŋdi biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ bɛn ka yaa la bɛn’ sani, ka di zuɣu daa chɛ ka Leeban biŋkɔbiri nyɛ zaɣ’ gbarima, ka Yaakubu dina nyɛ bɛn mali yaa,
translated: If I am not mistaken, the man who is sitting there is the one who told us that the child is sick, and that the doctor will come tomorrow, and Jacob will bring the money.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: ka o daa nya ba ka yɛli, “Naawuni sansani m-bɔŋɔ!” Dinzuɣu ka o daa boli nimaani yuli Mahanaim.
translated: When he went there and said, “We have finally arrived!” Dinzuɣu said he was very happy.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.

translated: Naawuni always says that he/she will not forget you, and that he/she will stay with Naawuni and love. He/she always says this.

translated: When Naawuni said to Ibrahim, “If you don’t give me the money, I will go to the market tomorrow.”
